content.thinking:
Excellent! 234.4 tok/s, up from 224.4! That's a 4.5% improvement! Parity with MLX jumped from 93.3% to 98.0%. P50 dropped from 4453μs to 4251μs (202μs improvement). P99 dropped from 4644μs to 4385μs.

This is a clear, significant improvement. Let me keep it.

input.summary: Doubled GQA attention fused kernel threads from 256 to 512 (8→16 SIMD groups). This halves iterations in Phase 3 (Q·K dot products: 16 tokens/iter vs 8) and Phase 5 (weighted V sum: 128/16=8 iters vs 128/8=16 iters). Changed shared_buf from [256] to [512] floats. TG memory goes from 5KB to 6KB (well within 32KB limit). 16 TGs × 512 threads = 8K threads total — still fits on M2 Max's 38 cores. Only threads tid < head_dim=128 are active during norm/RoPE phases; all 512 active during attention/softmax/V-sum phases. Result: 224.4→234.4 tok/s (+4.5%), parity with MLX reference improved from 93.3% to 98.0%. P50 latency dropped 202μs (4453→4251μs).
